samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal conditions, a motor vehicle lane light should be green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it is shortened to 10 clocks. If it is less than 10 clocks, it remains unchanged. The lane light and the sidewalk light should be paired. When the lane light is green or yellow, the sidewalk light is red. When the lane light is red, the sidewalk light is green. For simplicity, only the lane light is considered.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="red">Red signal output.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow signal output.</output>
        <output id="green">Green signal output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1_red">State 1, red signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2_yellow">State 2, yellow signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3_green">State 3, green signal.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>State transition logic.</description>
            <output id="state">State output.</output>
            <input id="state">State input.</input>
            <input id="cnt">Counter input.</input>
            <case>
                <when expression="state == idle">Transition to s1_red.</when>
                <when expression="state == s1_red">Transition to s3_green after 3 clock cycles.</when>
                <when expression="state == s2_yellow">Transition to s1_red after 3 clock cycles.</when>
                <when expression="state == s3_green">Transition to s2_yellow after 3 clock cycles.</when>
            </case>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>Counter logic.</description>
            <output id="cnt">Counter output.</output>
            <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
            <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
            <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
            <input id="green">Green signal input.</input>
            <input id="cnt">Counter input.</input>
            <if>
                <expression>if (!rst_n)</expression>
                <then>Set counter to 10.</then>
                <else_if expression="pass_request && green">Set counter to 10.</else_if>
                <else_if expression="!green && p_green">Set counter to 60.</else_if>
                <else_if expression="!yellow && p_yellow">Set counter to 5.</else_if>
                <else_if expression="!red && p_red">Set counter to 10.</else_if>
                <else>Decrement counter by 1.</else>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>Output logic.</description>
            <output id="red">Red signal output.</output>
            <output id="yellow">Yellow signal output.</output>
            <output id="green">Green signal output.</output>
            <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
            <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
            <input id="p_red">Previous red signal.</input>
            <input id="p_yellow">Previous yellow signal.</input>
            <input id="p_green">Previous green signal.</input>
            <assign>
                <description>Assigns p_red to red, p_yellow to yellow, p_green to green.</description>
                <output>red</output>
                <output>yellow</output>
                <output>green</output>
                <input>p_red</input>
                <input>p_yellow</input>
                <input>p_green</input>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>
            The traffic light controller logic is implemented using three sequential always blocks. 
            The first always block handles the state transitions based on the current state and the counter value. 
            The second always block updates the counter based on various conditions, such as reset, pass request, and signal states. 
            The third always block assigns the output signals (red, yellow, green) based on the previous values of the signals.
        </description>
    </implementation>
</module>
